meta.disclaimer: Platform positions are summarized from official party platforms and public statements. Always consult primary sources.

positions.title: Opposition to Palestinian State
positions.summary: Opposes the establishment of a Palestinian state west of the Jordan River. Advocates for Israeli security control over all territory between the river and the sea.

positions.title: Judicial Reform
positions.summary: Supports reforms to rebalance power between the Knesset and the judiciary, including changes to the override clause and judicial appointments committee.

positions.title: Free-Market Economy
positions.summary: Favors privatization, reducing business regulation, and encouraging foreign investment to stimulate growth.

positions.title: Settlement Expansion
positions.summary: Supports continued building in West Bank settlements and opposes settlement freezes as a precondition for negotiations.

positions.title: Strong Military Response in Gaza
positions.summary: Advocates for continued military pressure until Hamas is dismantled and all hostages are returned.

positions.title: Status Quo on Religion and State
positions.summary: Generally maintains the status quo arrangement on religious affairs, including Shabbat observance laws, as part of coalition agreements.

positions.title: Two-State Solution in Principle
positions.summary: Supports the framework of a two-state solution as a long-term goal, contingent on Palestinian recognition of Israel as a Jewish state and security guarantees.

positions.title: Ultra-Orthodox Military Conscription
positions.summary: Strongly advocates for mandatory IDF service for ultra-Orthodox men, viewing it as a matter of equal civic burden-sharing.

positions.title: Civil Marriage
positions.summary: Supports introducing civil marriage as an alternative to the rabbinate-controlled marriage system.

positions.title: Judicial Independence
positions.summary: Strongly opposes judicial reform legislation, advocating for an independent judiciary as a cornerstone of Israeli democracy.

positions.title: Hostage Deal Priority
positions.summary: Places return of hostages as the top security priority, even if it requires pausing military operations.

positions.title: Cost of Living Reform
positions.summary: Proposes reducing food and housing costs through breaking up monopolies, increasing competition, and reforming import regulations.

positions.title: Public Transit on Shabbat
positions.summary: Supports running public transportation on Shabbat in major cities, framing it as a matter of economic access and secular civic freedom.

positions.title: Israeli Sovereignty over All of Judea & Samaria
positions.summary: Advocates for full Israeli annexation of the West Bank. Opposes any Palestinian political sovereignty in the land of Israel.

positions.title: Jewish Law as Foundation of State Law
positions.summary: Believes Israeli law should be guided by halacha (Jewish law) in relevant domains, particularly family law and public life.

positions.title: Encouragement of Arab Emigration
positions.summary: Has proposed incentivizing Arab citizens and Palestinian residents to voluntarily emigrate, a position widely condemned as discriminatory.

positions.title: Settlement as National Mission
positions.summary: Views Jewish settlement throughout the Land of Israel as a religious and national imperative, with large budget allocations to settlements.

positions.title: Opposition to LGBTQ+ Legal Rights
positions.summary: Opposes same-sex adoption, surrogacy rights, and legal recognition of same-sex partnerships under religious grounds.

positions.title: Two-State Solution
positions.summary: Committed to a negotiated two-state solution based on 1967 borders with agreed land swaps, as the only path to lasting peace.

positions.title: Welfare State & Workers' Rights
positions.summary: Supports strengthening trade unions, raising the minimum wage, expanding social safety nets, and improving labor protections.

positions.title: Universal Healthcare Strengthening
positions.summary: Advocates for increased public investment in hospitals, reducing out-of-pocket medical costs, and expanding mental health coverage.

positions.title: Separation of Religion and State
positions.summary: Advocates for civil marriage, public transit on Shabbat, and reducing the Orthodox rabbinate's control over personal status law.

positions.title: Equal Civic Participation for Arab Citizens
positions.summary: Supports full integration of Arab citizens into the economy and public service, with targeted investment in Arab communities.

positions.title: Climate Action Plan
positions.summary: Proposes binding carbon reduction targets, investment in renewable energy, and phasing out coal by 2030.

positions.title: Immediate Ceasefire & Negotiations
positions.summary: Calls for an immediate ceasefire in Gaza, the return of all hostages through negotiation, and a political horizon for Palestinian statehood.

positions.title: Full LGBTQ+ Equality
positions.summary: Supports same-sex marriage, joint adoption, and full legal equality for LGBTQ+ individuals, including surrogacy rights.

positions.title: Complete Separation of Religion and State
positions.summary: Advocates for ending state funding of religious institutions, abolishing the rabbinate's monopoly on marriage, and fully secular public space.

positions.title: Progressive Taxation
positions.summary: Proposes a wealth tax and higher marginal income tax rates to fund public services and reduce inequality.

positions.title: Arab-Jewish Coexistence
positions.summary: Strongly supports equal rights for Arab citizens, Arabic as a genuinely co-equal official language in practice, and joint Arab-Jewish political projects.

positions.title: Green New Deal for Israel
positions.summary: Proposes major public investment in renewable energy, free public transit, and binding emissions law as climate emergency response.

positions.title: Yeshiva Student Draft Exemptions
positions.summary: Firmly opposes mandatory IDF conscription for yeshiva students, viewing Torah study as an equal national contribution.

positions.title: Religious Marriage Law
positions.summary: Defends the rabbinate's sole authority over Jewish marriage and divorce in Israel. Opposes civil marriage.

positions.title: Mizrahi Community Investment
positions.summary: Prioritizes budgets for Sephardi and Mizrahi communities, including religious schools (talmudei torah) and community centers.

positions.title: Social Welfare for Large Families
positions.summary: Advocates for generous child allowances and subsidized housing targeted at large religious families.

positions.title: No Public Transit on Shabbat
positions.summary: Strongly opposes any public transportation on Shabbat, viewing it as a violation of the Jewish character of the state.

positions.title: End of Occupation
positions.summary: Calls for full Israeli withdrawal from the occupied territories and establishment of a Palestinian state alongside Israel based on 1967 borders.

positions.title: Equal Rights for Arab Citizens
positions.summary: Advocates for full equality in law and practice for Arab citizens, including combating systemic discrimination in housing, employment, and policing.

positions.title: Bilingual State Services
positions.summary: Demands that all state services, signage, and official communications be fully available in Arabic as well as Hebrew.

positions.title: Workers' Rights & Anti-Privatization
positions.summary: Opposes privatization of public utilities, advocates for strong labor law enforcement, and supports expanding the public sector.

positions.title: Secular Democratic State
positions.summary: Advocates for a state based on civic rather than ethnic or religious identity, with full separation of religion and state.

positions.title: Dismantling of Nuclear Arsenal
positions.summary: Calls for a nuclear-free Middle East and Israeli participation in non-proliferation treaties.
